title: The golden age of oil lamp craft
body: Victorian craftsmen across Britain and Europe perfect the Duplex burner, borosilicate glass shade, and the precision brass fittings that define the golden era of oil lamp design. The specifications set in this era will not be improved upon.

title: Original tooling forged
body: The presses, dies, and lathes used to produce Duplex burners are precision-engineered in Birmingham. The alloy specifications and glass formulas are locked in. They will not change.

title: Collecting becomes an obsession
body: A passion for antique oil lamps grows into something more: the recognition that critical components have simply disappeared from the market. If no one is making them properly, perhaps someone should.

title: Original Birmingham tooling acquired
body: The original 100-year-old presses, dies, and tooling for the Duplex burner — from the Birmingham workshops where they were first made — are purchased and shipped to Melbourne, Australia. Nothing is recut. Everything runs as-is.

title: The Duplex Burner is made again
body: Running on the original Birmingham tooling in Melbourne, production of the Duplex Burner resumes exactly as it was manufactured over a century earlier. Even the Duplex wicking — produced here in Melbourne — carries the OLC mark.

title: Shades, fonts & glassware
body: Shades, fonts, chimneys, borosilicate glassware, and brassware — including Miller Flame Spreaders — are manufactured in India using moulds and tooling owned outright. Over twenty years of production. Every piece carries the OLC brand.

title: North American launch
body: Acme Vintage Supply brings the full catalog to North America for the first time. Original tooling. Owned moulds. Borosilicate glass. The same craft that has supplied Australian collectors for two decades — now available here.
